lines.text: Tucker here. I'm positioned on Atwood Avenue, East Side, about {{ELAPSED}} into tonight. Madison-wide count is holding at 38 cumulative kills. That's 19 in the last hour. Year-over-year, we're running about 11 percent hot for this point.

The neighborhood watch on Willy Street has their barricade holding solid at the Yahara bridge. I can see the sandbag line from here. No breach attempts in the last twenty minutes, but activity's concentrating north of State Street now — coordinated group movement, maybe 25 to 30 individuals, heading toward the Capitol Square perimeter.

Bladed weapons are seeing significant uptick. At least 9 of the 38 tonight are melee — that's 23.7 percent, up from the 2168 average of 22 percent at this hour. Cheaper to maintain. Quieter. Makes sense.

Tenney Park sector is quieter than expected. Lakefront exposure usually pulls higher numbers, but I'm only reading 4 incidents over there so far. Either the cold's keeping people indoors or there's territorial consolidation I'm not seeing on the scanner yet.

Wait — I'm getting a cluster notification on my feed. Beltline corridor near Todd Drive just lit up. Looks like a vehicle situation, multiple — hold on, let me get closer, the audio's degrading, I need to reposition, I'm moving north along the —

tickerItems.text: Madison YTD: 38 kills ({{ELAPSED}} elapsed). +11% vs 2168 same period.
East Side: 18 kills. Capitol Square perimeter: organized movement detected, 25-30 individuals, heading north.
Willy Street barricade: holding. Tenney Park: 4 incidents. Beltline: vehicle cluster reported, details pending.
PurgeShield structural panels. Last year 4,200 Madison units deployed. Your barricade deserves better than furniture. Call 1-800-SHIELDUP.
